project: Girls In Sports — Sprint 1: Smart Video Segmentation & Accurate Scoring
description: Vastly improve analysis accuracy and media clipping so every uploaded video is automatically decomposed into meaningful, individually-scored highlight clips before downstream curation or rendering.

stories.title: Smart Video Segmentation During Ingest
acceptance: Source videos >20 seconds are segmented into child CLIP Assets during INGEST_CLIP
Each child Asset has valid parentAssetId, startTimeMs, endTimeMs, durationSeconds, motionLevel, dominantMode
Child clips are individually queued for SCORE_CLIP jobs
Original source Asset status set to INGESTING while children process, then SCORED when all children complete
Videos ≤20 seconds produce a single child clip (passthrough)
Minimum clip length configurable (default 4s); very short segments merged with adjacent clips
Maximum clips per source: 20 (configurable); excess segments merged by motion-profile proximity
stories.notes: Implemented idempotency guard, lowered threshold from 60s to 20s, and set motionLevel/dominantMode on child Asset creation. Full smart segmentation (merging by motion profile, speaker-aware boundaries, audio-driven segmentation) is deferred to future refinement.

stories.title: Dual-Path Transcription with Venice Beta Primary + Whisper Fallback
acceptance: transcribeVideo() tries Venice beta /audio/transcribe first
If beta succeeds with diarized segments: speaker labels stored alongside transcript words
Coach-speaker heuristic: directive words + longer utterances classified as coach
If beta fails: fall back to Whisper /audio/transcriptions (current behavior preserved)
Fallback logged with quality flag transcriptionFallback: true
Diarized speaker info feeds into audioScore: coach-identified speech weighted 2× vs unidentified
Quality flag records transcriptionProvider: venice-beta | whisper-fallback
stories.notes: Implemented dual-path: primary call requests diarization via diarize=true/diarize_audio=true params. Parses speaker segments from Venice response. If no speaker segments returned, falls back to standard Whisper. Coach weighting implemented in computeAudioScore: keyword matches in coach-identified speakerSegments get +1× bonus. transcriptWordsJson persisted on Asset. transcriptionProvider and speakerSegmentsJson stored in ClipScore.

stories.title: Transcript-Driven Speech Clip Segmentation
acceptance: STT word-level timestamps + motion LOW for ≥3s activates speech segmentation mode
Sentence-boundary detection (silence gaps ≥0.8s or punctuation) defines clip boundaries
Diarized speaker changes trigger segment boundaries
Each speech clip carries transcriptExcerpt scoped to its time window
Speech clips classified as ClipType.SPEECH
Audio signal score applied at 70% for SPEECH clips (vs 30% visual)
stories.notes: Implemented in ingest-clip.ts: runs transcription on source video, stores full transcriptWordsJson on parent Asset. mergeSegmentBoundaries() merges ffmpeg scene cuts with STT silence gaps (≥0.8s) and speaker changes. Each child Asset gets scoped transcriptWordsJson for its time window. score-clip.ts now reads existing transcriptWordsJson from Asset and reconstructs TranscriptionResult, skipping redundant transcription API calls.

stories.title: Content-Aware Dynamic Frame Sampling
acceptance: ACTION/MIXED clips sampled at 3 fps (capped at 120 frames)
SPEECH/MONTAGE clips sampled at 1 fps (capped by duration)
Absolute maximum: 120 frames per clip
Batch size increased from 3 to 6 frames per Venice API call
If duration × fps exceeds cap, density reduced proportionally with evenly-spaced coverage
Frame extraction uses ffmpeg -ss seek with absolute timestamps
MAX_FRAMES_PER_CLIP configurable via env var (default: 120)
stories.notes: Replaced fixed frame counts with tiered FPS strategy: ACTION/MIXED at 3 fps, everything else at 1 fps. frameCount = min(duration × fps, MAX_FRAMES_PER_CLIP). If capped, interval = duration / frameCount for evenly-spaced coverage. batchSize increased from 3 to 6 in analyzeKeyframesWithVision. extractKeyframes now accepts interval parameter instead of computing internally.

stories.title: Audio-Signal Rescue & Speech Scoring
acceptance: Low-motion clips with high-signal audio identified and scored with boosted audio weight
Speech clips use 70/30 audio/visual weight split
Coach speech segments weighted 2× vs background speech
Crowd roar / celebration detected via audio energy peaks
Audio-driven composite score computed independently from motion score
stories.notes: Added detectCrowdRoar() using ffmpeg volumedetect filter — mean > -15dB or max > -3dB triggers hasCrowdRoar. SPEECH clips now bypass tiered scoring and use 70/30 audio/production weight split. Low-motion (<30) + high-audio (>50 or hasCrowdRoar) clips get audioSignalRescue=true and clipType promoted to MIXED. hasCrowdRoar and audioSignalRescue fields added to ClipScore schema and Immich tags.

stories.title: User-Selectable Music Model
acceptance: Campaign creation UI shows music model selector: minimax-music-v26 (default), elevenlabs-music [expensive - $1.25], minimax-music-v2, minimax-music-v25
Model selection stored in Campaign.musicModel
generate-music.ts reads Campaign.musicModel and calls correct endpoint
If selected model fails, fallback to minimax-music-v26 (never ace-step)
stories.notes: Campaign schema already had musicModelPreference field. UI added pill selector in event page Creative Brief panel with 4 models. API validates against allowlist and defaults to minimax-music-v26. generate-music.ts reads campaign.musicModelPreference, tries that model first, falls back to minimax-music-v26 on failure. Removed old hardcoded elevenlabs-music → ace-step fallback.
